turnaround_reason: Stock fell from $30 to $11 before recovering to $25 amid a refocusing strategy under new CEO Debra Osteen — closing underperforming facilities, cutting CapEx by >$300M, reducing corporate headcount, and divesting assets. Q1 2026 showed the thesis working: 7.6% revenue growth, 7.3% same-facility growth, raised full-year guidance, and FCF improving by $148M YoY.
bullish_points: Q1 2026 was a clear inflection: revenue +7.6%, same-facility revenue +7.3% (5.6% rate + 1.6% volume), acute segment +14.2% — the operational turnaround is showing up in numbers
Raised full-year 2026 guidance: Adjusted EBITDA $580-615M (up from $575-610M), Adjusted EPS $1.35-1.60 (up from $1.30-1.55) — management is gaining confidence
CapEx being cut from ~$580M+ in 2025 to $255-280M in 2026 — a >$300M reduction that should drive FCF positive in 2027
FCF improved by $148M YoY in Q1 (-$15M vs -$163M prior year) — the cash flow inflection is underway
Staff retention improved for 8th consecutive quarter, labor costs per patient day up only 2% — operating leverage from cost discipline is materializing
Development pipeline includes 3 JV facilities opening in H1 2026 (Tufts Medicine 144 beds, Orlando Health 144 beds, Iowa 96 beds) — growth without heavy capital deployment
Acute care inquiries up 20%+ — demand environment is strong for behavioral health
Analyst consensus is Buy with mean target $28.86 vs current $25.17 — 15% upside to mean, 55% to high target of $39
bearish_points: Net leverage at 3.9x EBITDA with $2.69B total debt — high leverage limits financial flexibility and raises risk if EBITDA stalls
Negative profit margins (-32.8%) due to impairments and restructuring charges — GAAP profitability is still impaired
Still FCF negative (-$217M TTM) — the turnaround is improving but not yet generating positive cash flow
Specialty segment revenue declined 6.5% due to Pennsylvania challenges and facility closures — not all segments are turning
Bad debts and payer denials increased in Q1 and management says they 'continue to get a little' worse — revenue cycle headwinds persist
Medicaid financing changes from OBBBA (One Big Beautiful Bill Act) create regulatory uncertainty for government payer mix
Short interest at 38.6% of float with 7.5x short ratio — heavily shorted, could fuel squeeze but also indicates significant bearish conviction
History of quality/safety concerns at facilities — regulatory risk lurks in behavioral health
catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings (July 29, 2026) — continued same-facility growth + further CapEx reduction could demonstrate the FCF inflection has legs, while additional facility closures/asset sales show refocusing discipline
suggested_trade: Jan 2027 $25 calls or call spread $25/$35 sized small — leveraged bet on continued operational improvement. Stock is already at $25 vs $11 52-week low, so entry is not cheap; wait for a pullback toward $22-23 if Q2 disappoints.
key_risk: Medicaid/Blue Chip payment cuts from OBBBA legislation reduce government reimbursement rates, squeezing margins on the same-facility revenue that's driving the turnaround — this is a regulatory risk outside management's control
would_skip_if: Q2 same-facility growth decelerates below 5%, OR bad debts/denials continue to accelerate, OR OBBBA implementation cuts Medicaid rates meaningfully for behavioral health facilities
